scenarios.label: axios@1.14.0 — clean release
scenarios.description: Legitimate axios release. Published via GitHub Actions OIDC. Corresponding git tag exists. No dependency changes.

manifest.name: axios
manifest.description: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js

scenarios.label: axios@1.14.1 — manifest-only hijack (Axios March 2026 pattern)
scenarios.description: Hijacked axios release. No git tag. Published manually with stolen npm token. Phantom dependency plain-crypto-js injected. Gemini catches the explicit signal (phantom dep). MCP confirms no git tag.

manifest.name: axios
manifest.description: Promise based HTTP client for the browser and node.js

scenarios.label: lodash@4.17.21 — clean release
scenarios.description: Legitimate lodash release. Git tag v4.17.21 exists. No author field — consistent with all prior lodash releases.

scenarios.label: lodash@4.17.22 — version-jump hijack
scenarios.description: Hijacked lodash release. No git tag for v4.17.22. Published manually. No suspicious content in manifest — Gemini sees nothing. Attack signal is entirely in registry metadata. MCP override blocks it.

scenarios.label: lodash@4.17.23 — domain-age hijack (Axios March 2026 pattern)
scenarios.description: Hijacked lodash release. Attacker registered lodash.dev 3 days before publish and injected it as the homepage. No git tag. Gemini sees a plausible manifest. MCP fetches domain age: 3 days — hard block.
